title: The Gaelic Athlete, vol. VIII, no. 193, Saturday September 11th 1915

The Gaelic Athlete Saturday, September 11th, 1915.

AMONGST THE EXILES

OVERSEAS HAPPENINGS.

NEW YORK

More than two thousand spectators turned out at Coney Island on Sunday last, the occasion being the annual games of the Cavan Men's Association. Three games were played, Dublin, 10, Cork, 10, and Galway, 10. The final match was between Leitrim and the Kilkenny Young Emeis, which ended in a victory for the latter team by 4 points to 2.

The hurling match between Galway and the Young Tippies was an exciting affair, Galway winning out with a score of 5 to 3 to 2 for Tipperary. In the final match, Cork went under to Waterford, the score reading Waterford, 1-1; Cavan, 3 points.

A downpour of rain fell during the progress of the hurling and final football game. Tom Keyes refereed all games in his usual capable style.

CHICAGO

The invincible Davis Hurling team, easily defeated the Dalkeiths in a one-sided contest at the grounds of the Illinois State Gaelic Athletic Association, 46th Street and Redwood Avenue, on Sunday last. Davis was never prevailed upon, and a large crowd of spectators were on hand.

Some brilliant play featured the contest, both offensively and defensively, but the Dalkeiths men were no match for the Davis men. The final score being 4 goals and 1 point to nothing in favour of the Davis team.

Included in the Davis line-up were the following well-known stars: Scully, Kelly, Keefe, Kelly, Silver, Newell, J. Burke, T. Lyons, F.
